model.description: xAI • Large reasoning model designed for real-time information access, coding, and general problem solving.

pick.rationale: SMU ranks significantly higher than Miami (OH) in both KenPom (42 vs 93) and Torvik (42 vs 87), boasting elite adjusted offense from a much tougher ACC schedule compared to the MAC. Miami (OH)'s 31-1 record is impressive but inflated by weak competition, while SMU's experience against high-major foes provides a clear advantage in this play-in game. Betting markets reflect this with SMU as a 6.5-point favorite, and no major injuries or recent news alter the metrics favoring the Mustangs.

reasoningStep.title: Team Quality and Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Advanced metrics strongly favor SMU over the hot but lower-ranked Miami (OH).
evidence: SMU as 6.5-pt favorite from multiple previews

The Miami RedsHawks are going to the NCAA Tournament for the first time in 19 years. Miami is in the NCAA Tournament as an at-large team for the first time since 1999, when Miami reached the Sweet 16 behind star Wally Szczerbiak. Miami will face fellow No. 11 seed SMU, an at-large team from the Atlantic Coast Conference. For the first time in 19 years, the Miami RedHawks are headed to the NCAA men's basketball tournament. Miami, in the tournament for the first time since 2007, earned an at-large bid to March Madness for the first time since Wally Szczerbiak led the RedHawks to the 1999 Sweet 16. Led by Mid-American Conference Coach of the Year Travis Steele , the RedHawks completed an undefeated regular season and entered Selection Sunday with a 31-1 record. The RedHawks weren't able to secure an automatic-bid from the MAC conference tournament title , but their impressive regular-season resume earned them a No. 11 seed in the Midwest region. modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
